title: eriogonum ovalifolium subsp. ochroleucum
intro: Eriogonum ovalifolium subsp. ochroleucum, also known as the white sagebrush or ghost buckwheat, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Eriogonum ovalifolium subsp. ochroleucum include its ability to support respiratory health, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote wound healing. Eriogonum ovalifolium subsp. ochroleucum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
